faqs.question: How can I get into P.S.P.S.'s Indira Institute of Pharmacy for UG/PG courses? Am I eligible for admission?
faqs.answer:
P.S.P.S.'s Indira Institute of Pharmacy has below criteria for taking admissions to it's various courses:
Courses Eligibility
D.Pharma Candidate passed 10+2 (science stream) with Physics, Chemistry and Biology or Mathematics or Any other qualification approved by the Pharmacy Council of India from any recognised institute/board are eligible to apply
B.Pharma Candidate must have studied Physics and Chemistry as compulsory subjects along with one of the Mathematics/Biology subjects with above mentioned marks in the above subjects taken together from any recognised Board/Council. The mentioned marks of SC/ST and OBC is for the candidate belonging to maharashtra State. Candidates who are appearing at the qualifying examination are also eligible. Maharashtra State candidates must have to appear for MHTCET. All India Candidates need to appear for MHTCET/NEET. However, preference shall be given to the candidate obtaining non zero positive score in NEET over the candidates who obtained non zero score in MHTCET.

reviews_list.review_title: The college was great, and I was satisfied with the course.

review_content.Placements: 70% of students were placed from the pass-out batch. During that placement, the highest package was 13 LPA, and the lowest was 2.5 LPA I seen in that time. The top recruiting company was Cipla Pharmaceuticals. Almost all students got internships through the course.
review_content.Infrastructure: The college campus was good, and it was located in a beautiful natural setting with a good climate. The college provided free Wi-Fi facility around the campus for students. Labs were well-equipped, and classrooms were top-notch. The library also contained a large stock of books related to the course and other subjects. The college provided all the professional facilities required for the best course outcome.

review_content.Placements: When I passed out, I got 68%. I was offered a package of 5 LPA. Pharmacy is a good branch to serve public health and help others by gaining knowledge to manage public health. At least 75% of students had the opportunity to work. The highest package was 13 LPA and the lowest was 2.5 LPA.
review_content.Infrastructure: The hostel had a good surrounding area, and rooms were very clean and spacious. There was no congestion in the area. A study table, light, fan, Wi-Fi, and water purifier were available 24/7, along with electricity. Security was also provided. The canteen was good and affordable, offering very good food. In the library, all books were available, and e-classrooms were also available for convenient study. The staff were very senior about studies and very professional.

reviews_list.review_title: It is the best college for B. Pharmacy course.

review_content.Placements: We had so many placements in our college that everyone can find a job with a good salary package. The average salary package of pharma student is 1.8 LPA, and the highest salary package in our college is 3.0 LPA. The majority of the students were placed in various companies in Goa and Mumbai. The final-year students were eligible for campus placements. Around 60-80% of students either get placed or get good internships. TCS, Cognizant, IQVIA, and Episource companies had visited the college.
review_content.Infrastructure: Our institution had a fantastic infrastructure and a library where we can acquire a lot of books to read. Our college sports were really good and we are now building a lot more acres. During the course, the campus provided a favorable and healthy environment in which to learn and develop skills of choosing. The classrooms were large and well-equipped with air-conditioning. The library was handy for any reference books on any of the compulsory subjects that we may require. An auditorium was available for events. There were laboratories where students can practice formulation and other pharma skills.

reviews_list.review_title: Review of P.S.P.S.'s Indira Institute of Pharmacy.

review_content.Placements: About 50% of the students get placed from here. There is a placement cell in our college, which mainly focuses on providing chances for students in well-known companies. Our college also tries to make most of the students get internships in well-known companies. The placement cell mainly focuses on this, and you can rely on them.
review_content.Infrastructure: There is Wi-Fi in our college, and the infrastructure is also fine. Laboratories are also well-equipped. All machinery required for pharma practicals is available in our college. I suggest that you can take admission to this college.

review_content.Placements: A very big percentage of students from Emcure, Sun Pharma, and Cipla colleges passed. Several students did their internships in top companies. The college infrastructure was very good. A large number of people graduated from these IIP colleges.
review_content.Infrastructure: Labs were very good. The college provided different types of facilities for students, including well-developed laboratories, good Wi-Fi connection, and well-developed classrooms. The college also provided a good canteen and medical facilities.

reviews_list.review_title: Good college for pharmacy and good infrastructure.

review_content.Placements: Placement of this college is about 3 LPA to 5 LPA for the Diploma course, which is good for freshers. The placement cell is very supportive and helpful to make you ready for a job. Some students get internships in many pharma companies. The college has tied up with many companies to ensure good placements.
review_content.Infrastructure: Classrooms and labs are well-furnished and fully air-conditioned, which makes them comfortable. The library of this college is decent, and books are updated by their authors. The canteen of this college is decent, as it costs a reasonable price for every item. But there is a need for improvement in hygiene in the canteen.

reviews_list.review_title: Good faculty members and practical labs, average campus life, needs stronger placement support.

review_content.Placements: Placement of Signa College of Pharmacy was rated excellent for D.Pharma by a verified student of the 2024 batch. Average salary was around 3 LPA - 5 LPA. It provided strong campus placement support. Internships were also offered with pharma firms. Good for freshers, and it provided many more opportunities in pharmacy field.
review_content.Infrastructure: College had morden labs, smart classrooms, good library, and practical setup for B.Pharma. Hostel was decent with Wi-Fi, clean room, and mess food. Overall, campus was average in size but neat and student friendly. The library of the college was average in size, and the availability of books was enough.
